meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve Project Abandonment
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning your SaaS projects. Learn to implement strategic pricing using Antigravity and Claude Code to ensure your first app is a success.
content.answer_block: The root cause of project abandonment is often a lack of perceived value. By defining your SaaS pricing early, you force yourself to focus on a specific customer problem. This tutorial shows you how to integrate Stripe and pricing logic, turning your code into a professional, revenue-ready product.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: the initial excitement of a new app idea, the rush of the first few commits, and then the slow, painful drift into the 'graveyard of unfinished projects.' You aren't failing because you lack coding skills; you are failing because you lack a market anchor. When a project doesn't have a price tag, it doesn't have a purpose.

Today, we break that cycle. We are going to treat your student project like a real-world SaaS. By integrating a pricing strategy into your architecture from day one, you transition from 'hobbyist coder' to 'technical founder.' Let’s transform your repository from a neglected folder into a launch-ready asset.

steps.title: Define Your Value Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the unit of value your users pay for. This prevents scope creep by strictly defining what is 'paid' vs 'free'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project requirements and suggest a value-based pricing model that limits scope to MVP features only.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Generate a pricing-schema.json that maps features to tiers: Free, Pro, and Enterprise.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Stripe Integration
steps.description: Integrate the payment gateway early. This forces you to handle authentication and database state, which are the foundations of any real-world app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Stripe checkout session handler using the pricing tiers defined in our schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Install stripe and create an api/checkout_sessions route for handling subscription logic.'

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating
steps.description: Use your pricing model to toggle UI components. This ensures users only see what they pay for, keeping your codebase clean and modular.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-order component or hook to gate UI features based on user subscription status from the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Refactor the dashboard to hide pro features using the new useSubscription hook.'

steps.title: Automate Webhook Listeners
steps.description: Handle subscription events like upgrades or cancellations. This turns your app into a robust system that manages itself.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure webhook handler to sync Stripe event statuses to my local user database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Create a webhook endpoint to update user status in the database on payment_succeeded.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Ship your project. Nothing kills momentum like a project that never sees the light of day. Deploying makes it real.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment configuration file for Vercel/Railway that includes all necessary environment variables for Stripe.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Generate a deployment.yaml and verify all secrets are masked.'

pro_tips: Always start with a 'Pay-per-use' model to avoid the complexity of recurring billing in your first project.
Use a 'Feature Flag' pattern for pricing tiers to make your code easier to test without needing a live Stripe instance.
Never hardcode prices; fetch them from the Stripe API metadata so you can pivot without redeploying your app.
Build a 'Pricing Table' UI component that is purely data-driven to maximize reusability across your portfolio.
Focus on a single, high-pain 'Pro' feature to justify your first tier; don't try to build a full suite of tools immediately.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, focus on acquiring your first five beta users to validate your logic and refine your feature set.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop dreaming and start shipping. Don't let this code sit in a private repo; push it live, get your first payment, and build the career you deserve. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
